claim: Global data center electricity consumption is projected to roughly double by 2030, creating localized grid integration pressure.
notes: Authoritative demand context; not specific to gas generators.

claim: In faster data-center demand scenarios, grid queues and power availability can increase reliance on fossil generation.
notes: Supports gas as bridge/backup option, while also highlighting renewables in base case.

claim: U.S. electricity demand is forecast to post its strongest four-year growth since 2000, fueled by data centers.
notes: Demand is power-system-wide, not generator-specific.

claim: A high data-center demand scenario would primarily increase utilization of natural gas-fired plants in the U.S.
notes: Supports natural gas as marginal supply response.

claim: Uptime Institute identifies unprecedented giant data-center power plans, with onsite/off-grid generation and natural gas among preferred options.
notes: Industry authority; likely paywalled details.

claim: GE Vernova and Chevron are targeting 4 GW of natural-gas power by 2027 for AI data center demand.
notes: Company official; direct gas-power/data-center link.

claim: Cummins says power availability is a gating factor and operators are exploring onsite generation, hybrid systems and microgrids.
notes: Company official; supports demand and supply-chain pressure.

claim: Cummins reports robust demand for data center backup power and continued strength in data center power generation markets.
notes: Direct company operating evidence.

claim: Wartsila received a 412 MW U.S. hyperscale data center gas-engine order and says total U.S. data-center engine capacity sold exceeds 1.6 GW.
notes: Direct order evidence.

claim: Mitsubishi Power allocated two M501JAC turbines for 1,150 MW dedicated onsite data-center power at Cheyenne Power Hub.
notes: Direct project/turbine allocation evidence.

claim: Siemens Energy and Eaton describe a modular data-center power plant concept using SGT-800 gas turbines, redundancy and battery storage.
notes: Company official; solution concept rather than order.

claim: Shanghai Electric has a gas-turbine JV and distributed-energy gas turbine technology, but direct AI data-center order evidence was not found in this pass.
notes: Candidate evidence is capability-based, not order-based.

claim: Jereh Gas provides gas generator sets, aero-derivative gas turbines and power services for data centers, with strategic partnerships with Jenbacher and Siemens.
notes: Relevant product exposure; listed parent needs revenue/order verification.

claim: Rehlko positions gas gensets and hybrid energy solutions for hyperscale AI data centers.
notes: Private company/brand; useful comparable but not direct listed target.

claim: MHI offers data center one-stop solutions including gas turbines and emergency generation.
notes: Listed Japanese comparable; direct data-center solution exposure.

claim: Howmet produces industrial gas turbine components including airfoils, hot-section coatings, rings, disks and forgings.
notes: Component-level overseas listed comparable; not data-center-specific.

claim: Oerlikon provides turbine hot-section coating solutions that improve corrosion and oxidation resistance and extend blade service life.
notes: Coating/process layer evidence.

claim: First-stage IGT blades face high stress, creep, hot corrosion and high temperature; coating/process solutions are relevant to component life.
notes: Supports hot-section bottleneck logic.

claim: Yingliu Group has casting, HIP, machining, spraying, master alloy and ceramic-core capability, serving gas-turbine high-end equipment.
notes: A-share component supplier mapping; revenue split still needs verification.

claim: MHI Dongfang Gas Turbine Guangzhou is described as a domestic base for M701F-class gas turbine core hot-section components and maintenance service.
notes: Direct hot-section manufacturing and repair evidence, but JV not separate listed target.

claim: Gangyan Gaona materials and products include high-temperature alloys and gas-turbine-related turbine disks/protection parts.
notes: Materials/component mapping; current revenue mix needs update.

claim: Wanze 2024 annual report includes single-crystal superalloy blade R&D and industrialization for aero engines and gas turbines.
notes: High-temperature alloy blade capability; direct data-center exposure not shown.

claim: Tunan is described as having cast and wrought superalloy production capability used in aero engines and gas turbines.
notes: Materials layer mapping; direct fuel-to-data-center linkage absent.

claim: High-temperature alloy turbine blades are core hot-section components for power-generation and industrial gas turbines.
notes: Technical bottleneck evidence, not company-specific.
